title: Bat Removal & Exclusion in Prospect Lefferts Gardens, Bro...
metaDescription: Professional bat removal and exclusion services in Prospect Lefferts Gardens, Brooklyn. Safe, humane bat control for homes and buildings. Call Best Pest Control NYC today!
intro: Prospect Lefferts Gardens homeowners trust Best Pest Control NYC for safe, humane bat removal and exclusion services. Our certified technicians specialize in removing bats from attics, chimneys, and wall voids while ensuring they don't return. We provide comprehensive solutions tailored to the unique architecture of Prospect Lefferts Gardens homes.
whyUs: Best Pest Control NYC has over 15 years of experience serving Prospect Lefferts Gardens with licensed, insured bat removal specialists who follow all NYC wildlife regulations. We offer same-day emergency service, thorough inspections, and guaranteed exclusion work to protect your Victorian-era home or multi-family building from future bat infestations.
localContext: The historic Victorian and brownstone homes throughout Prospect Lefferts Gardens, particularly near Prospect Park and along tree-lined streets like Maple and Midwood, often provide ideal entry points for bats seeking roosting sites. The neighborhood's proximity to Prospect Park creates a favorable environment for bat populations, making professional exclusion services essential for protecting these beautiful century-old structures.

faqs.question: How much does bat removal cost in Prospect Lefferts Gardens?
faqs.answer: Bat removal and exclusion services in Prospect Lefferts Gardens typically range from $900 to $2,500, depending on the size of the colony, number of entry points, and complexity of the exclusion work required. We provide free inspections and detailed estimates before beginning any work.

faqs.question: Is bat removal legal in NYC?
faqs.answer: Yes, but it must be done humanely and in compliance with New York State wildlife laws. Bats are protected species, so removal must be performed by licensed professionals using exclusion methods that allow bats to leave but not re-enter. Certain times of year, particularly during maternity season (May-August), have restrictions to protect flightless young bats.

faqs.question: How do I know if I have bats in my Prospect Lefferts Gardens home?
faqs.answer: Common signs include squeaking or scratching sounds at dusk or dawn, droppings (guano) in attics or near entry points, brown staining around gaps in siding or rooflines, and seeing bats enter or exit your home at sunset. You may also notice a strong ammonia-like odor from accumulated guano.

faqs.question: How long does the bat exclusion process take?
faqs.answer: The complete bat exclusion process typically takes 2-4 weeks. After the initial inspection and sealing of entry points with one-way exclusion devices, we monitor for 1-2 weeks to ensure all bats have left. Once confirmed, we seal all remaining openings and perform cleanup and decontamination of affected areas as needed.
